title: arthraxon ciliaris subsp. nudus
intro: Arthraxon ciliaris subsp. nudus, also known as naked arthraxon, is a species of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and improving digestion, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant can be dried and used as a tea, or made into a powder for use in capsules or tinctures.
